message:
feat(cli): emit progress updates during --refresh indexing

Follow-up to 3e4e6ef4. The initial --refresh implementation ran
indexer::run_index synchronously with no output beyond a single
"Refreshing index..." stderr line. A 30-second index pass then
produced a silent 30-second wait before the TUI opened or search
ran, which users read as a hang.

Fix: spawn run_index on a dedicated thread and poll the shared
IndexingProgress atomics from the main thread. Emit a one-line
plain-text status on stderr whenever phase/current/total changes
(matches the "Progress emitted inline (same as cass index
--progress plain)" line from #198's spec).

Also adds a catch for thread panics so a panicking indexer still
falls through to the caller (spec: "fall through to the main
action on the stale index rather than blocking the user"). The
ordinary Ok/Err paths keep their existing semantics — Ok prints
"done.", Err prints the warning and returns — so the non-fatal
contract is unchanged.

No new dependencies. No changes to the CLI surface. No changes
to default behaviour (still off without --refresh).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
